rationale.root_cause: In CWE415_Double_Free__malloc_free_char_01_bad(), data is malloc'ed (around line 29) and then freed twice (first at ~32, then again at ~34), causing a double free. The second free uses the same stale pointer after the first free returned the memory to the allocator.
rationale.local_fix: Remove the second free(data) call so the buffer is freed exactly once, or set data = NULL immediately after the first free to prevent a second free from acting on a stale pointer.
rationale.global_fix: Adopt a single-owner cleanup pattern so each allocation has exactly one responsible free site (e.g., an owner wrapper with a destroy function or unique ownership semantics). This centralizes deallocation and prevents multiple frees of the same allocation.
